triggerInternet: 🌐 I saw something on the Internet
calmNow: I need to calm down now
stopQuestion: Will what you are looking for now help you or feed your anxiety?
impulseNeverUsed: You haven't used this exercise yet.
objectiveEmotionalLink: On days when you completed more goals, your emotional rating was {{score}}/10.
emotionalMemoryImproving: Your recent days show a positive trend.
emotionalMemoryChallenging: Your recent days seem to have been more challenging.
emotionalMemoryStable: Your stability has remained consistent over the days.
emotionalMemoryMornings: Your mornings seem to be the more positive moments of your day.
emotionalMemoryBalanced: Your balance remains similar throughout the day.
emotionalMemoryNotEnoughData: Keep recording to discover patterns in your mind.
emotionalMemoryAverage: In recent days your average was {{score}}/10.
emotionalMemoryAfternoons: Your afternoons seem to be calmer than your mornings.
emotionalMemoryObservation: Observe your journey. Each record helps you understand your patterns better.
goal1: Drink a glass of water slowly, noticing its freshness
goal2: Practice 1 minute of slow deep breathing
goal3: Write down 3 simple things you are grateful for today
goal4: Take a short walk outside for 5 to 10 minutes
goal5: Do some gentle neck and shoulder stretches
goal6: Send a short caring message to a friend or family member
goal7: Eat a piece of fruit mindfully, without digital distractions
putPhoneAway: Put your phone away for a few minutes
progressDescription: Your well-being deserves to be tracked. See how your mood evolves and celebrate your achievements.
needTwoDaysMessage: You need at least two days of Morning and Afternoon ratings to display your progress chart.
hugPhrase1: Breathe deeply... Hold... Exhale slowly. You are here and now.
hugPhrase2: This moment of tension will pass. Your body knows how to calm down.
hugPhrase3: You don't have to solve everything now. Focus only on your next breath.
hugPhrase4: Feel your feet firmly on the ground. You are strong and capable of overcoming this.
hugPhrase5: Allow your shoulders to relax. Let go of the weight that is not yours.
hugPhrase6: Your worth is not defined by how much you do today.
hugPhrase7: Anxiety is just a passing cloud in the sky of your mind.
hugPhrase8: You did the best you could today, and that is enough.
hugPhrase9: Breathe peace... release tension... you are in a safe space.
groundingSuccessMessage: Anxiety is a natural reaction that decreases when we connect with simple and organic elements. You did an excellent job calming your body today.
holdBreathMessage: Hold for a moment without air, relaxing your mind...
earthDescription: Anxiety creates fast thoughts. Let's force the mind to connect with the real physical and natural space around you:
windBreathingIntro: Let's imitate the calm and continuous rhythm of the wind to restore inner balance. Focus on the circle below:
windInhale: Slowly breathe in through your nose, imagining a fresh breeze...
windHold: Hold the freshness in your chest and relax your shoulders...
windExhale: Release the warm air and all tension through your mouth...
earthSeeQuestion: Do you see something green or organic around you?
earthTouchQuestion: Touch a natural texture that you can reach
earthHearQuestion: Try to focus and listen to a sound in the environment
treeGroundingText: Imagine your body as a strong tree, with invisible and deep roots anchored to the ground. The wind blows strongly through your leaves, but your trunk remains stable and firm. Let the wind pass... you are safe.
breathingExplanation: Oxygen is the body's fastest natural remedy. With this rhythmic breathing, you have told your heart directly that there is no danger. You control your rhythm.
sighInstruction: Take one final long and noisy sigh through your mouth... Let everything go!
waterExplanation: Cold water triggers the "Diving Reflex", automatically activating regulating nerves that slow the heart rate. Try one of these simple actions:
waterTip: Tip: Feeling the cold of ice or washing your hands also helps a lot!
riverMetaphor: Anxiety works like a restless river. Do not try to stop the flow or swim against the current, because it only tires you. Simply float and let the current pass. Soon the water will become calm again.
sunExplanation: Physical tension builds up without us noticing. Imagine warm sunlight touching your body and relax these three main areas:
warmthMetaphor: Imagine your whole body receiving the gentle warmth of a spring day. Tension melts away like snow under the sun. You are here, alive, safe, and completely calm.
feelChestWarmth: Feel the warmth in your chest
feetGrounded: Feel your feet firmly on the ground
choiceDescription: Don't fight the feeling. Let's choose a natural and gentle way to anchor you to the present:
friendConnectionReward: Your companion felt your effort to connect and earned
thoughtNeverHappened: This has never happened to me.
thoughtNever: This has never happened to me.
justificationPhrase: I noticed that when "{{trigger}}" happened, your mind reacted with {{emotion}} under the thought that "{{thought}}". Remember that this is only a moment of strong alert from your system, not a real fact. Your body is simply trying to protect you. Breathe and welcome this signal positively: it shows how much you care about yourself, but you are safe now.
psychoInternet: We noticed that the impulse started after seeing information on the Internet. When we are anxious, it is very common to search for answers online. Although this may bring immediate relief, it also increases the chance of finding worrying information.
psychoSymptom: We noticed that it started after feeling a physical symptom. When there is health anxiety, our body becomes hypervigilant, turning small normal sensations into danger signals.
psychoDiseaseTalk: We noticed that the trigger was hearing conversations about health or illnesses. Our brain immediately connects these stories to our own wellbeing, activating the alert system.
psychoDefault: We cannot always identify what triggered the impulse. This is completely normal.
cycleExplanation: Every time you interrupt this cycle, you teach your brain that you can handle anxiety without constantly seeking reassurance.
paperExercise: Now take a sheet of paper and a pen. Write a detailed gratitude thought about something positive that genuinely lifts you up (for example: a special moment with your child, a warm hug, or something good from your day).
cycleExplanation: Every time you interrupt this cycle, you are teaching your brain that you can handle anxiety without constantly seeking reassurance.
gratitudeExercise: Now take a sheet of paper and a pen. Write a detailed gratitude thought about something positive that genuinely lifts you up.
stage1Desc: A small glowing egg that absorbs your calm until it hatches.
mindFocusCompleted: Your mind was actively in positive focus. Your friend gained
readBookDesc: Turn off notifications and focus on the words of a physical or digital text.
readBookExerciseDesc: Choose a chapter or text that interests you. Read slowly, paying attention to how your eyes follow each sentence. If your mind wanders, gently return to the text.
readBookTip: Prefer reading on paper to reduce blue light and screen stimulation
writeDesc: Release accumulated thoughts by freely putting them onto paper.
writeExerciseDesc: Write everything that comes to mind without judging, correcting mistakes, or deleting. It can be a gratitude list, your current worries, or simply describing your room.
writeTip: Handwriting helps slow down the speed of anxious thoughts
drawDesc: Use shapes, lines, and colors to anchor your attention through creativity.
drawExerciseDesc: Take paper and a pen. Draw repeated geometric shapes, mandalas, or try sketching a simple object right in front of you.
drawTip: Focus on the physical sensation of the pencil touching the paper
organizeDesc: Organizing the outside helps create a feeling of inner order.
organizeExerciseDesc: Choose a small area: organize your desktop icons, clean your downloads folder, delete old emails, or tidy the drawer where you keep pens.
organizeTip: Start with a small space. Small actions create great mental peace
craftDesc: Synchronize your brain with precise fine motor tasks.
craftExerciseDesc: Try making a simple paper boat or airplane, tying small knots, or carefully cleaning a small object. Feel the texture of each fold and the precision of the movement.
craftTip: Physical touch and precision effort interrupt the cycle of mental rumination
plantsDesc: Interact with natural elements and living beings to slow down your pace.
plantsExerciseDesc: Water your plants, carefully clean dust from their leaves, or separate seeds. If you do not have plants, observe a tree through the window and notice its details.
plantsTip: Biophilia (connection with nature) has been shown to reduce cortisol levels
stage2Desc: Still tiny, it loves deep breathing and fresh water.
stage3Desc: It has grown wings of light and loves exploring peaceful feelings.
stage4Desc: With magical ears and a fluffy tail, it protects you from restless thoughts.
stage5Desc: A celestial guardian meditating on a cloud of tranquility.
wellbeingDescription: Your wellbeing fluctuates. Record your morning and afternoon to analyze patterns in your history.
guardianEvolution: By taking care of yourself, you help your guardian gain energy. Your Friend evolved successfully!
extraReward: You earned +30 extra evolution reward points!
dailyNote: Note or feeling about your day (Optional):
crisisStartSupport: Tap to start immediate crisis support
avatarWelcome: Hello! I'm your calm companion. Let's breathe together. 💚
affirmation1: I'm really proud of your progress today! 🌸
affirmation2: Take a deep breath with me... Everything is okay. 🌿
affirmation3: Every small step you take is a great victory! ✨
affirmation4: Remember to drink some water and stretch your shoulders today. 💧
affirmation5: I'm so happy when we take care of each other! 🥰
affirmation6: Anxiety is only a cloud; you are the whole sky. ☁️
affirmation7: Don't be so hard on yourself. You're doing your best. ☀️
affirmation8: I'm glad you're here taking care of your mind! 🧘‍♂️
mindHugDescription: A 5-minute refuge designed to slow down your body. Focus on your breathing and calmly read the messages.
startHugMessage: Click start to begin your hug and calm your thoughts.
dailyGoals: Easy and realistic goals for today. Each goal gives points to your Friend!
sessionCompletedMessage: You made an excellent investment in your mental health today! You earned
impulseStep1: Step 1: What is the current intensity of your impulse? (1 to 10)
impulseStep3: Step 3: What emotion is accompanying this impulse?
impulseStep4: Step 4: What is the main thought in your mind?
impulseStep8: Step 8: How do you rate your intensity now after the exercise?
whatToAchieveToday: What do you want to achieve today?
goalPlaceholder: Ex: Read 2 pages of a book, water the plants...
shareSuccess: Thank you for sharing! Your message was sent to the sharing panel.
sharePlaceholder: Describe your feelings or victories today in a comforting way... Ex: 'Today I felt my heartbeat rise, but I remembered to close my eyes and count to 10...'
goalsDescription: Easy and realistic goals for today. Each goal gives points to your Friend!
impulseCongratulations: Congratulations for welcoming your mind and managing your impulse. You earned 30 XP!
sosDescription: Do you feel an impulse or anxiety? Let's focus and manage this together.
repeatBreathing: Do this two more times before moving forward.
groundingDescription: 5-3-1 sensory grounding focused on natural elements around you.
boxBreathingDescription: Rhythmic box breathing to oxygenate and slow down your heartbeat.
coldWaterDescription: Activation of the vagal reflex with the calming physiological power of cold.
sunRelaxationDescription: Progressive muscle relaxation simulating the comforting warmth of sunlight.
earthExample: Ex: A plant, sunlight, the sky, a flower...
coldWaterActionFace: I washed my face or wrists with cold water
coldWaterActionDrink: I drank a glass of cold water slowly
jawInstruction: Part your teeth slightly and relax your mouth.
shouldersInstruction: Let your shoulders drop away from your ears.
handsInstruction: Open your fingers and place them on your leg.
petCompanion: Tap your companion to give it some affection (+1 Point)
soundExample: Ex: Wind outside, your heartbeat, breathing, silence...
Example: Wind outside, your heartbeat, breathing, silence...
sunRelaxationDescription: Progressive muscle relaxation simulating the comforting warmth of sunlight.
coldWaterActionFace: I splashed cold water on my face or wrists
warmRelease: Warm Up and Melt Away Tension
shouldersInstruction: Let your shoulders drop away from your ears.
handsInstruction: Open your fingers and rest them on your leg.
